description: Fragment of a beautifully written ketubba. Groom: Netanʾel. Bride: Mubāraka. The trousseau list appears to be written in rhymed Hebrew. The sum 250 dinars is named. The name Moshe appears also in the first line, maybe the father of the bride or groom. Reused on verso for accounts. (Information in part from Goitein's index card.)
description_he: שבר כתובה כתובה בצורה יפה; חתן: נתנאל; כלה: מבארכה; רשימת הנדוניה בעברית מחורזת; סכום: 250 דינרים
